text: @collidecapital just announced a $95 million fund. I have the scoop on what that means, and what comes next.  @poetryaaron @brian_hollins

As a founder or any team builder, diversity is best built at the start. As Taskrabbit founder @leah_solivan learned, procrastination leads to weaker teams and a harder effort later.

We dive into all of her expert tips for builders and founders in the latest episode of our podcast Build Mode with @hellabella__

Taskrabbit founder @leah_solivan makes an obvious case for diversity at your startup: it drastically expands who knows how to reach a wider market.

Get more of her expert insights in the latest episode of our Build Mode podcast with @hellabella__

For women entering the founding and startup ecosystem, Taskrabbit founder @leah_solivan has a wealth of insights, especially on why you shouldn’t hold yourself back. Listen in on the latest episode of Build Mode with @hellabella__ wherever you catch up with your pods.

You can outsource HR.

You can’t outsource culture.

At an early-stage startup, culture is what you tolerate and what you call out.

In the latest episode of Build Mode with @hellabella__ , Isaiah Granet, co-founder of Bland, breaks down why founders have to own culture from day one.

Time is the most valuable resource in an early stage startup.

And hiring the wrong person can cost invaluable weeks, as Isaiah Granet, co-founder of Bland, told @hellabella__ on our Build Mode podcast.

Listen in to the latest episode, and subscribe to the rest, wherever you get your podcasts!

There is a new black fund ready to deploy capital! Get ready for BKR Capital (@bkrcapital) Fund II. 

 #startups #venture #founder #dei

At the earliest stages, investors focus on two things: Is the idea big enough, and are you the best person to build it? Everything else comes down to trust in the founder to make the right decisions along the way.

Listen to Hala Jalwan and Alessio Tresanti, co-founders of Rivio on our Build Mode podcast with @hellabella__

Conflict between founders isn’t always a bad thing. When handled well, it can lead to breakthroughs, as Ian Schmidt, strategic advisor at Trimergence, explores in the latest episode of Build Mode with @hellabella__

Arena ranks frontier LLMs...while also being backed by companies vying to top the leaderboards.

How do they stay neutral? Co-founder Anastasios Angelopoulos breaks it down on their claims of algorithmic neutrality on our Equity Podcast.
A robotic Olaf from Frozen brings #NVIDIAGTC’s keynote with CEO Jensen Huang to a close, showcasing a partnership between Nvidia, Disney, and DeepMind AI.
